success_surface: Multi-formatting spreadsheet manipulation requiring font changes, conditional value capping, overflow distribution, and type-sensitive number formatting
effective_behaviors: Apply global styling uniformly across entire worksheet before targeted modifications
Use direct cell-level APIs for precise value and format control when complex logic is needed
Implement conditional value splitting logic with threshold comparison and adjacent column overflow
Apply type-aware number formatting (whole vs decimal) dynamically per cell
Process defined row ranges explicitly rather than entire columns for efficiency
Wrap numeric conversions in exception handling to gracefully skip invalid data
Consistently apply formatting rules across all relevant target columns
evidence: Agent used ws.iter_rows() loop to apply '等线' font to every cell in the worksheet before data manipulation
Used ws.cell() for row-by-row access to read values, apply threshold logic (>40), split values between adjacent columns
Checked float(val).is_integer() to conditionally apply 'General' or '#,##0.##' format per cell
Defined start_row=7, end_row=23 explicitly instead of iterating full column indefinitely
Wrapped n_float = float(n_val) in try/except block to handle non-numeric cells safely
Applied identical formatting logic pattern to columns N, O, and P consistently

memory_items.title: Global Sheet Styling Before Data Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Apply universal formatting (fonts, colors, borders) to all cells before performing targeted value changes to ensure consistency
memory_items.generalization: When both sheet-wide styling and specific cell logic are required, execute styling loops first, then proceed to data transformation operations

memory_items.title: Threshold-Based Value Distribution Pattern
memory_items.lesson: For capping values at thresholds, calculate original minus cap and write remainder to adjacent column
memory_items.generalization: When splitting values based on thresholds: store capped value in source column, compute and place overflow in destination column

memory_items.title: Type-Sensitive Number Formatting
memory_items.lesson: Detect integer vs decimal values programmatically and apply appropriate format codes accordingly
memory_items.generalization: Check numeric type properties (is_integer) before applying number_format to preserve clean display of whole numbers while maintaining precision for decimals

memory_items.title: Explicit Range Bounding for Row Processing
memory_items.lesson: Define clear start and end row indices when manipulating specific data regions
memory_items.generalization: Bound row iterations with explicit ranges to avoid unintended modifications to header rows or empty trailing rows

memory_items.title: Robust Numeric Conversion Handling
memory_items.lesson: Wrap all value-to-number conversions in exception handlers to skip malformed data
memory_items.generalization: Always use try/except blocks around float/int conversions to prevent crashes from non-numeric cell contents

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Multi-Format Sheet Manipulation Patterns

**Global Styling First**: When both universal formatting (font, colors) and specific cell logic are required, complete all sheet-wide style operations BEFORE targeted data transformations. This ensures styling baseline is established before values change.

**Threshold Capping & Overflow Distribution**: For tasks requiring value caps:
1. Read original value
2. Compare against threshold
3. Write capped value back to source cell
4. Calculate (original - threshold) as overflow
5. Write overflow to designated adjacent column

**Dynamic Type-Aware Formatting**: Apply number formats conditionally:
- Check if numeric value is integer using .is_integer()
- Apply 'General' format for whole numbers
- Apply custom format (e.g., '#,##0.##') for decimals
- Perform check AFTER any value modification to format final state

**Precision Range Control**: Always define explicit start_row and end_row when processing data regions instead of iterating entire columns. This protects headers and trailing empty rows.

**Safe Type Conversions**: Wrap all float()/int() conversions in try/except blocks. Non-numeric cells should be skipped gracefully without breaking execution.
